verdict.answer_first: B: sentence 1 directly states 'A database index is a separate data structure...to speed up lookups' before details; A also starts well but adds analogy and complexity sooner.
verdict.decisiveness: B: gives a clear rule, 'Add an index when reads on a predictable column are the bottleneck,' then says measure with 'EXPLAIN ANALYZE'.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it gives the practical rule and checklist without extra edge-case detail; its short version is 'index the columns you frequently search, join, or sort on in large tables'.
